pg9-p1-s2: It was years since I could remember his coming to New York in that condition.
pg9-p1-s4: Rumor had it that he had been committed for alcoholic insanity.
pg9-p1-s5: I wondered how he had escaped.
pg9-p1-s6:
Of course he would have
dinner, and then I could drink openly with him.
pg9-p1-s7:
Unmindful of his welfare, I thought only of recapturing the spirit of
other days.
pg9-p1-s8: There was that time we had chartered an airplane to complete a jag!
pg9-p1-s9:
His coming was an oasis in this dreary
desert of futility.
pg9-p1:
come over. He was sober. It was years since I could remember his coming to New York in that condition. I was amazed. Rumor had it that he had been committed for alcoholic insanity. I wondered how he had escaped. Of course he would have
dinner, and then I could drink openly with him. Unmindful of his welfare, I thought only of recapturing the spirit of
other days. There was that time we had chartered an airplane to complete a jag! His coming was an oasis in this dreary
desert of futility. The very thing - an oasis! Drinkers are like that.
pg9-p2-s1: The door opened and he stood there, fresh-skinned and glowing.
pg9-p2-s2: There was something about his eyes.
pg9-p2:
The door opened and he stood there, fresh-skinned and glowing. There was something about his eyes. He was inexplicably
different. What had happened?
pg9-p3-s1: I pushed a drink across the table.
pg9-p3-s3: Disappointed but curious, I wondered what had got into the fellow.
pg9-p3:
I pushed a drink across the table. He refused it. Disappointed but curious, I wondered what had got into the fellow. He
wasn't himself.
pg9-p4: "Come, what's this all about?" I queried.
pg9-p5-s2: Simply, but smilingly, he said, "I've got religion."
pg9-p5: He looked straight at me. Simply, but smilingly, he said, "I've got religion."
pg9-p6-s2: So that was it - last summer an alcoholic crackpot; now, I suspected, a little cracked about religion.
pg9-p6-s4: Yes, the old boy was on fire all right.
pg9-p6-s5: But bless his heart, let him rant!
pg9-p6-s6:
Besides, my gin
would last longer than his preaching.
pg9-p6:
I was aghast. So that was it - last summer an alcoholic crackpot; now, I suspected, a little cracked about religion. He
had that starry-eyed look. Yes, the old boy was on fire all right. But bless his heart, let him rant! Besides, my gin
would last longer than his preaching.
pg9-p7-s2:
In a matter of fact way he told how two men had appeared in court, persuading the judge to
suspend his commitment.
pg9-p7-s3: They had told of a simple religious idea and a practical program of action.
pg9-p7-s4:
That was two months
ago and the result was self-evident.
pg9-p7:
But he did no ranting. In a matter of fact way he told how two men had appeared in court, persuading the judge to
suspend his commitment. They had told of a simple religious idea and a practical program of action. That was two months
ago and the result was self-evident. It worked!
pg9-p8-s1: He had come to pass his experience along to me - if
pg9-p8: He had come to pass his experience along to me - if
